original_hypothesis.title: isFreeze Modifier Prevents Users from Recovering Tokens During Freeze
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The unlistVesting function uses the isFreeze modifier. If the admin freezes the marketplace, users are completely blocked from unlisting and recovering their vested tokens. Even the admin cannot unlist on their behalf.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Emergency pause mechanisms should typically pause new deposits/listings but allow withdrawals/unlistings to prevent holding user funds hostage.

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Pause modifier applied to withdrawal function
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Users cannot recover funds during a freeze
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Separate pause state for listings vs unlistings

analysis_questions: Is this intended behavior according to the protocol design?
validation_requirements: Attempt to unlist as user and as admin

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Admin freezes the marketplace during an emergency. Users panic and try to unlist their tokens to secure them, but all unlist transactions revert. Funds are trapped until admin unfreezes.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Temporary loss of access to funds

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests for unlisting during freeze

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
